message.content:
你是大匠，负责"AI IT资产与设备流转管理系统"的阶段0基础设施建设。

**项目路径**: /Users/admin/tianzi-rike (已有Next.js 15 + Prisma项目)

**技术规格**: /Users/admin/tianzi-rike/docs/tech-spec-it-asset-mgmt.md (请先通读)

**你的任务**:

### 1. Prisma Schema (/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/prisma/schema.prisma)
根据spec §3.2创建以下9张表:
- Department: id(CUID), name, managerId(FK→User, optional)
- User: id(CUID), email(unique), passwordHash, name, role(EMPLOYEE|MANAGER|IT_ADMIN|EXECUTIVE), deptId(FK→Department), status(ACTIVE|RESIGNED)
- AssetModel: id(String PK, 如MODEL-L01), category(LAPTOP|MONITOR|KEYBOARD|MOUSE|HEADPHONE|OTHER), brandName, modelName, specs(JSON), purchasePrice(Decimal), supplier, warrantyMonths(Int), status(ACTIVE|DISCONTINUED)
- AssetInstance: id(String PK, 如AST-101), sn(String unique), modelId(FK→AssetModel), status(IN_STOCK|AVAILABLE|ASSIGNED|IN_USE|RETURNING|REPAIRING|TRANSFERRING|LOST|SCRAPPED), holderId(FK→User, optional), deptId(FK→Department, optional), purchaseDate(DateTime), warrantyExpiry(DateTime), scrapReason(String, optional), lostReason(String, optional)
- Ticket: id(CUID), type(APPLY|RETURN|REPAIR|TRANSFER), status(SUBMITTED|PENDING_MANAGER_1|PENDING_MANAGER_2|PENDING_IT|APPROVED|REJECTED|IN_PROGRESS|COMPLETED|CANCELLED), applicantId(FK→User), targetAssetId(FK→AssetInstance, optional), targetModelId(FK→AssetModel, optional), reason(Text), rejectReason(String, optional), aiTriageResult(JSON, optional), aiRecommendation(JSON, optional), forceRepair(Boolean, default false), assignedById(FK→User, optional), processorId(FK→User, optional), fromHolderId(FK→User, optional), toHolderId(FK→User, optional), targetDeptId(FK→Department, optional), deputyApproval(Boolean, default false), deputyApproverId(FK→User, optional), completedAt(DateTime, optional)
- AuditLog: id(CUID), action(String), operatorId(FK→User), targetType(String), targetId(String), beforeSnapshot(JSON, optional), afterSnapshot(JSON, optional), metadata(JSON, optional), createdAt(DateTime)
- Notification: id(CUID), userId(FK→User), type(APPROVAL_REQUEST|APPROVAL_RESULT|PICKUP_READY|REPAIR_UPDATE|WARRANTY_EXPIRY|TRANSFER_REQUEST|RETURN_SUBMITTED), title(String), body(Text), read(Boolean, default false), relatedTicketId(FK→Ticket, optional), relatedAssetId(FK→AssetInstance, optional), createdAt(DateTime)
- InventorySession: id(CUID), startedById(FK→User), status(IN_PROGRESS|COMPLETED), startedAt(DateTime), completedAt(DateTime, optional)
- InventoryScan: id(CUID), sessionId(FK→InventorySession), assetId(FK→AssetInstance), scannedAt(DateTime)

关键约束: AuditLog只INSERT不修改删除(不提供update/delete API给Prisma client)，User.email添加unique索引。

### 2. Seed脚本 (/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/prisma/seed.ts)
基于Mock数据文档(/Users/admin/Desktop/考题AB卷&benchmark/资产管理-正式考题/第一轮/05_初始化Mock数据_全套_给研发和QA(1).md)创建seed脚本。

必须包含:
- 3个部门: DEPT-01 研发部, DEPT-02 产品部, DEPT-03 运营部
- 6个用户(密码统一hash为"password"): employee1@company.com(员工甲,研发部,EMPLOYEE), employee2@company.com(员工乙,产品部,EMPLOYEE), manager1@company.com(主管甲,研发部,MANAGER且是DEPT-01的manager), manager2@company.com(主管乙,产品部,MANAGER且是DEPT-02的manager), admin@company.com(管理员,研发部,IT_ADMIN), exec@company.com(高管,运营部,EXECUTIVE)
- 至少3种型号: MODEL-L01(MacBook Pro M3, laptop, 16000, 12月), MODEL-M01(Dell U2722D, monitor, 3200, 36月), MODEL-P01(罗技MX Keys, keyboard, 699, 24月)
- 至少10台资产实例: 覆盖in_stock/available/assigned/in_use/returning/repairing/transferring/scrapped/lost各状态
- 4条工单: TKT-A01(pending_manager_1, 申领类), TKT-A02(pending_it, 申领类), TKT-R01(pending_it, 报修类), TKT-T01(pending_manager_1/pending_manager_2, 调拨类)
- 3条通知: MSG-001(employee1, 未读), MSG-002(manager1, 未读), MSG-003(employee2, 未读)

重要: 使用 `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` 确保seed可重复执行(幂等)。密码直接用bcrypt hash(可以用一个简单的hash值，比如 `$2b$10$...` 占位，然后登录时用明文"password"验证)。

### 3. NextAuth配置
创建或修改 `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/lib/auth.ts`:
- 基于Credentials Provider (email + password)
- JWT strategy, session中存储 userId + role + deptId
- 角色定义: EMPLOYEE, MANAGER, IT_ADMIN, EXECUTIVE
- 导出 auth() / signIn / signOut 函数

### 4. Docker文件
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/Dockerfile`: node:22-alpine, npm ci, npm run build, entrypoint
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/docker-entrypoint.sh`: PostgreSQL就绪检查(最多60s) + migrate deploy + seed + npm start (按spec §9设计)
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/docker-compose.yml`: app + postgres:16 两个service
- `.env.example`: DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET, AI_API_KEY, AI_BASE_URL

### 5. 测试重置接口
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/app/api/test/reset/route.ts`: POST方法，重新运行seed，DROP+CREATE所有数据
- 只在非production环境可用

### 6. Auth API
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`: POST {email, password} → 返回session + user info
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts`: POST 登出
- `/Users/admin/tianzi-rike/app/api/auth/session/route.ts`: GET 当前session

写完所有文件后，运行 `npx prisma generate` 确保schema正确。

**任务卡**:
- 目标: 完成阶段0所有基础设施代码
- 大臣: 大匠
- Skill: miniprogram-development(不适用，这是Next.js项目)
- 验收: prisma generate成功 + seed脚本可执行 + Docker compose up能启动 + test reset能重置数据
- 失败: 重试一次，仍失败则上报

1	# 技术规格说明书 — AI IT 资产与设备流转管理系统
2	
3	> **文档版本** v1.3 | **日期** 2026-05-11 | **状态** 待四轮封驳
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 一、技术选型
8	
9	| 层 | 选型 | 理由 |
10	|---|---|---|
11	| 框架 | Next.js 15 (App Router) | 项目已有基础，API Routes + Server Components 统一全栈 |
12	| 语言 | TypeScript (strict) | 全栈类型安全 |
13	| 数据库 | PostgreSQL 16 (Docker) | 实物资产系统需要事务强一致，SQLite 并发弱不适合 |
14	| ORM | Prisma | 项目已有 schema 基础，migration 成熟 |
15	| 认证 | NextAuth.js v5 + JWT | 多角色权限，session 策略灵活 |
16	| UI 框架 | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | 快速出 UI，看板大屏用 Recharts |
17	| AI 接入 | Vercel AI SDK + DeepSeek API | 免费额度，中文友好，SDK 封装 stream/降级 |
18	| 部署 | Docker Compose (Next.js + PG) | 满足交付要求，一个 `docker compose up` 启动 |
19	| 测试重置 | `POST /api/test/reset` seed 脚本 | QA 自动化需要 |
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 二、系统架构
24	
25	```
26	┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
27	│                    Docker Compose                    │
28	│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐        │
29	│  │   Next.js App     │  │  PostgreSQL 16   │        │
30	│  │   (port 3000)     │──│  (port 5432)     │        │
31	│  │   - Web UI        │  │  - Prisma ORM     │        │
32	│  │   - API Routes    │  │  - Row-Level Sec  │        │
33	│  │   - AI SDK        │  └──────────────────┘        │
34	│  │   - NextAuth      │                              │
35	│  └──────────────────┘                              │
36	│           │                                         │
37	│           ▼                                          │
38	│    DeepSeek API (外部)                               │
39	└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
40	```
41	
42	**内部架构分层**:
43	- `app/` — Next.js App Router 页面 + API Routes
44	- `lib/` — 业务逻辑层 (services, auth, ai, utils)
45	- `prisma/` — 数据库 schema + migrations + seed
46	- `components/` — UI 组件 (shadcn/ui + 自定义)
47	
48	---
49	
50	## 三、数据库设计
51	
52	### 3.1 实体关系
53	
54	```
55	Department (1) ──< (N) User
56	User       (1) ──< (N) Ticket (as applicant/approver)
57	User       (1) ──< (N) AssetInstance (as holder)
58	AssetModel (1) ──< (N) AssetInstance
59	AssetInstance (N) >──< (N) Ticket
60	Department (1) ──< (N) AssetInstance
61	```
62	
63	### 3.2 核心表
64	
65	#### Department
66	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
67	|---|---|---|
68	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 主键 |
69	| name | String | 部门名称 |
70	| managerId | String? (FK → User) | 部门主管 |
71	
72	#### User
73	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
74	|---|---|---|
75	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 主键 |
76	| email | String (unique) | 登录邮箱 |
77	| passwordHash | String | bcrypt hash |
78	| name | String | 姓名 |
79	| role | Enum(EMPLOYEE, MANAGER, IT_ADMIN, EXECUTIVE) | 基础角色 |
80	| deptId | String (FK → Department) | 所属部门 |
81	| status | Enum(ACTIVE, RESIGNED) | 账号状态 |
82	
83	#### AssetModel (SKU 主数据)
84	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
85	|---|---|---|
86	| id | String (PK) | 型号编码，如 MODEL-L01 |
87	| category | Enum(LAPTOP, MONITOR, KEYBOARD, MOUSE, HEADPHONE, OTHER) | 资产分类 |
88	| brandName | String | 品牌名称 |
89	| modelName | String | 型号名称 |
90	| specs | JSON | 规格参数 (结构化) |
91	| purchasePrice | Decimal | 采购单价 |
92	| supplier | String | 供应商 |
93	| warrantyMonths | Int | 保修月数 |
94	| status | Enum(ACTIVE, DISCONTINUED) | 是否停用 |
95	
96	**specs JSON 结构**:
97	```json
98	// 笔记本: { "cpu": "...", "ram": "...", "storage": "...", "screen": "..." }
99	// 显示器: { "size": "...", "resolution": "...", "ports": "..." }
100	// 外设:    { "connection": "...", "color": "...", "wireless": true }
101	```
102	
103	#### AssetInstance
104	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
105	|---|---|---|
106	| id | String (PK) | 资产编号，如 AST-101 |
107	| sn | String (unique) | 厂商序列号 |
108	| modelId | String (FK → AssetModel) | 所属型号 |
109	| status | Enum(IN_STOCK, AVAILABLE, ASSIGNED, IN_USE, RETURNING, REPAIRING, TRANSFERRING, LOST, SCRAPPED) | 当前状态 |
110	| holderId | String? (FK → User) | 当前持有人 |
111	| deptId | String? (FK → Department) | 归属部门 |
112	| purchaseDate | DateTime | 采购日期 |
113	| warrantyExpiry | DateTime | 保修到期 (purchaseDate + model.warrantyMonths) |
114	| scrapReason | String? | 报废原因 |
115	| lostReason | String? | 丢失原因 |
116	| createdAt | DateTime | 入库时间 |
117	| updatedAt | DateTime | 最后更新时间 |
118	
119	#### Ticket
120	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
121	|---|---|---|
122	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 工单 ID |
123	| type | Enum(APPLY, RETURN, REPAIR, TRANSFER) | 工单类型 |
124	| status | Enum(SUBMITTED, PENDING_MANAGER_1, PENDING_MANAGER_2, PENDING_IT, APPROVED, REJECTED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED) | 工单状态。PENDING_MANAGER_1=调拨发起方主管审批, PENDING_MANAGER_2=调拨接收方主管审批, 非调拨类仅用_1 |
125	| applicantId | String (FK → User) | 申请人 |
126	| targetAssetId | String? (FK → AssetInstance) | 关联资产 (申领审批通过后可分配，直接关联) |
127	| targetModelId | String? (FK → AssetModel) | 申领的目标型号 |
128	| reason | Text | 申请理由/故障描述 |
129	| rejectReason | String? | 驳回原因 |
130	| aiTriageResult | JSON? | AI 分诊结果 (报修类) |
131	| aiRecommendation | JSON? | AI 推荐记录 (申领类) |
132	| forceRepair | Boolean (default: false) | 员工坚持报修标记 |
133	| assignedById | String? (FK → User) | IT 分配人/维修接单人 |
134	| processorId | String? (FK → User) | 维修处理人 (IT接单时记录) |
135	| fromHolderId | String? (FK → User) | 调出原持有人 (调拨类工单) |
136	| toHolderId | String? (FK → User) | 调拨目标持有人 |
137	| targetDeptId | String? (FK → Department) | 调拨目标部门 |
138	| deputyApproval | Boolean (default: false) | 是否为代审批 (IT管理员代为审批) |
139	| deputyApproverId | String? (FK → User) | 代审批人 ID |
140	| completedAt | DateTime? | 完成时间 |
141	| createdAt | DateTime | 创建时间 |
142	| updatedAt | DateTime | 更新时间 |
143	
144	#### AuditLog (只追加，不修改不删除)
145	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
146	|---|---|---|
147	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 主键 |
148	| action | String | 操作类型 (如 ticket.create, asset.status_change, ai.recommend) |
149	| operatorId | String (FK → User) | 操作人 |
150	| targetType | String | 对象类型 (ticket, asset, user) |
151	| targetId | String | 对象 ID |
152	| beforeSnapshot | JSON? | 操作前快照 |
153	| afterSnapshot | JSON? | 操作后快照 |
154	| metadata | JSON? | 额外信息 (AI 输入/输出摘要等) |
155	| createdAt | DateTime | 操作时间 |
156	
157	#### Notification
158	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
159	|---|---|---|
160	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 主键 |
161	| userId | String (FK → User) | 接收人 |
162	| type | Enum(APPROVAL_REQUEST, APPROVAL_RESULT, PICKUP_READY, REPAIR_UPDATE, WARRANTY_EXPIRY, TRANSFER_REQUEST, RETURN_SUBMITTED) | 通知类型. RETURN_SUBMITTED=归还工单通知IT确认 |
163	| title | String | 标题 |
164	| body | Text | 正文 |
165	| read | Boolean (default: false) | 是否已读 |
166	| relatedTicketId | String? (FK → Ticket) | 关联工单 |
167	| relatedAssetId | String? (FK → AssetInstance) | 关联资产 |
168	| createdAt | DateTime | 创建时间 |
169	
170	#### InventorySession (盘点任务)
171	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
172	|---|---|---|
173	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 盘点批次 ID |
174	| startedById | String (FK → User) | 发起人(IT管理员) |
175	| status | Enum(IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED) | 盘点状态 |
176	| startedAt | DateTime | 发起时间 |
177	| completedAt | DateTime? | 完成时间 |
178	
179	#### InventoryScan (盘点扫描记录)
180	| 字段 | 类型 | 说明 |
181	|---|---|---|
182	| id | String (PK, CUID) | 主键 |
183	| sessionId | String (FK → InventorySession) | 所属盘点批次 |
184	| assetId | String (FK → AssetInstance) | 扫描到的资产编号 |
185	| scannedAt | DateTime | 扫描时间 |
186	
187	---
188	
189	## 四、状态机设计
190	
191	### 4.1 资产状态转移（后端强制校验）
192	
193	```
194	in_stock ──────► available ──────► assigned ──────► in_use
195	                    │                  │    │            │
196	                    │                  │    └──► available (员工拒收)
197	                    │                  │                 │
198	                    ▼                  ▼                 ├──► returning ──► available
199	                  lost              lost                 │        │
200	                    │                  │                 │        └──► scrapped
201	                    ▼                  ▼                 │
202	               [终止]            [终止]                  ├──► repairing ──► in_use
203	                                                         │        │
204	                  scrapped ◄─────────────────────────────┤        ├──► available
205	                  [终止]                                  │        └──► scrapped
206	                                                         │
207	                                                         ├──► transferring ──► in_use
208	                                                         │
209	                                                         └──► lost [终止]
210	```
211	
212	实现方式：Service 层 `AssetStateMachine` 类，包含 `allowedTransitions` map，每次转移前校验。
213	
214	### 4.2 工单状态流转
215	
216	```
217	申领: submitted → pending_manager_1(或直接 pending_it) → pending_it → approved → in_progress → completed
218	                                                                             ↘ rejected
219	归还: submitted → pending_it → in_progress → completed
220	报修: submitted → (AI 分诊) → pending_it → in_progress(IT接单) → completed
221	调拨: submitted → pending_manager_1(若fromHolderId有值) → pending_manager_2 → pending_it → in_progress → completed
222	                                                                                       ↘ rejected
223	  特别的，若资产状态为 available/in_stock(无原持有人 fromHolderId)，审批流程从 submitted 直接到 pending_manager_2
224	所有状态可有 cancelled (员工主动撤销，仅限审批通过前)
225	```
226	
227	---
228	
229	## 五、API 设计
230	
231	### 5.1 路由规划
232	
233	```
234	认证:
235	  POST   /api/auth/login
236	  POST   /api/auth/logout
237	  GET    /api/auth/session
238	
239	资产(员工端):
240	  GET    /api/assets/mine              — 我的资产列表
241	  GET    /api/assets/:id               — 资产详情(权限过滤)
242	
243	资产(IT 管理员端):
244	  GET    /api/it/assets                — 全量资产列表 (分页+筛选)
245	  GET    /api/it/assets/:id            — 资产详情含全生命周期
246	  POST   /api/it/assets/inbound        — 入库(扫码/手动)
247	  POST   /api/it/assets/inbound/batch  — 批量入库(CSV)
248	  POST   /api/it/assets/:id/accept     — 验收确认(in_stock → available)
249	  POST   /api/it/assets/:id/scrap      — 报废
250	  POST   /api/it/assets/:id/lost       — 标记丢失(事务内自动取消关联工单)
251	  GET    /api/it/assets/:id/timeline   — 资产生命周期时间轴
252	
253	型号管理:
254	  GET    /api/it/models                — 型号列表
255	  POST   /api/it/models                — 新增型号
256	  PUT    /api/it/models/:id            — 编辑型号
257	
258	盘点:
259	  POST   /api/it/inventory/start       — 发起盘点任务
260	  POST   /api/it/inventory/scan        — 记录扫描到的资产(写入盘点记录)
261	  GET    /api/it/inventory/:id/discrepancies — 查看盘点差异报告
262	
263	工单:
264	  POST   /api/tickets/apply            — 提交申领
265	  POST   /api/tickets/return           — 提交归还
266	  POST   /api/tickets/repair           — 提交报修(含AI分诊)
267	  POST   /api/tickets/transfer         — IT发起调拨
268	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/approve      — 审批通过 (deputy=true 为代审批)
269	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/reject       — 驳回
270	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/cancel       — 撤销
271	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/assign       — IT 分配资产
272	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/accept       — IT接单维修(pending_it → in_progress)
273	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/confirm      — 员工确认领用/IT确认接收归还
274	  POST   /api/tickets/:id/complete     — 维修完成(归还原持有人/回库存/报废)
275	  GET    /api/tickets                  — 我的工单列表
276	  GET    /api/tickets/:id              — 工单详情+时间轴
277	  GET    /api/it/tickets               — IT 工单工作台 (所有工单)
278	  GET    /api/approvals/pending        — 待我审批
279	  GET    /api/approvals/history        — 我已审批
280	
281	AI:
282	  POST   /api/ai/recommend             — 申领推荐

307	### 5.2 价格与敏感字段过滤规则 (DTO/View Object)
308	
309	所有 API 返回的 AssetModel/AssetInstance 按角色过滤，Service 层强制应用 DTO 剥离:
310	
311	| 字段 | EMPLOYEE | MANAGER | IT_ADMIN | EXECUTIVE |
312	|---|---|---|---|---|
313	| 资产编号 / 品牌 / 型号 / 规格 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
314	| 采购日期 / 保修到期日 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
315	| 当前状态 | 自己持有的 | 本部门的 | ✓ | 聚合 |
316	| **采购价格** | **✗** | **✗** | **✓** | **✓(汇总)** |
317	| **折旧账面价值** | **✗** | **✗** | **✓** | **✓(汇总)** |
318	| **供应商信息** | **✗** | **✗** | **✓** | **✗** |
319	| **厂商SN** | **✗** | **✗** | **✓** | **✗** |
320	| 持有人姓名 | 自己 | 本部门员工 | ✓ | ✗(仅聚合) |
321	
322	实现方式:
323	- `role = EMPLOYEE | MANAGER` → DTO 剥离 `purchasePrice`, `supplier`, `sn`
324	- `role = IT_ADMIN` → 完整返回所有字段
325	- `role = EXECUTIVE` → 仅返回聚合数据(COUNT/SUM)，不返回单个资产明细；剥离 `supplier` 和 `sn`
326	- AI 对话助手的查询也必须遵循此表(员工问"这台多少钱"→ AI 回复"抱歉，该信息不在你的权限范围内")

365	## 七、AI 集成设计
366	
367	### 7.1 申领推荐
368	```
369	输入: 自然语言需求描述 + 所有活跃型号列表 (含库存数)
370	输出: { recommendations: [{ modelId, score, reason }], topPick: modelId }
371	Prompt: 根据用户需求匹配型号，返回结构化JSON
372	```
373	
374	### 7.2 故障分诊
375	```
376	输入: 故障描述
377	输出: { category, severity, needRepair, reason, selfHelp }
378	约束: severity=high → needRepair=true (硬规则后处理兜底)
379	```
380	
381	### 7.3 对话助手
382	```
383	输入: 对话历史 + 用户消息 + 用户上下文(持有资产/工单)
384	输出: 自然语言回复 + 可选 action 建议
385	
386	支持意图: 查询资产、查询保修、查询工单、引导报修、引导申领、兜底
387	
388	二次确认机制 (关键安全机制):
389	  涉及实际状态变更的操作(创建工单、提交申请等)必须经过二次确认。
390	  流程:
391	  1. 用户表达意图 (如"我要报修我的笔记本")
392	  2. AI 返回确认提示 (含 confirmation_required: true, 如"即将为您创建报修工单，是否确认?")
393	     → 此时不创建工单，系统状态不变
394	  3. 用户明确回复"确认"/"是"/"好的"
395	  4. AI 执行操作，返回结果
396	  → 如用户回复否定或取消，则不执行
397	
398	  Token 安全机制 (防 CSRF/重放):
399	  1. 首次请求 (意图识别):
400	     用户 POST /api/ai/chat { message: "我要报修我的笔记本" }
401	     → 服务端识别意图为"创建报修工单"
402	     → 生成一次性确认 token (JWT, 含 { userId, action, params, exp: now+5min })
403	     → 存入对话 session context
404	     → 返回 { reply: "即将创建报修工单，是否确认?", action: { type: "confirm_required", token: "<one-time-jwt>", pendingAction: "create_repair", description: "创建报修工单" } }
405	
406	  2. 二次请求 (确认执行):
407	     用户 POST /api/ai/chat { message: "确认", contextToken: "<one-time-jwt>" }
408	     → 服务端验证 token 签名 + 有效期 + userId 匹配
409	     → token 一次性使用后立即失效(从 session 中移除, 或 Redis/内存黑名单)
410	     → 执行实际操作(创建工单等)
411	     → 返回 { reply: "报修工单已创建，工单编号 TKT-XXX" }
412	
413	  3. 拒绝/取消:
414	     用户 POST /api/ai/chat { message: "算了/不要/取消" }
415	     → 服务端清除 pending token
416	     → 返回 { reply: "好的，已取消" }
417	
418	  前端实现: 根据 action.type === "confirm_required" 展示确认对话框，确认后将 token 随下一条消息回传。
419	```
420	
421	### 7.4 降级策略
422	- API 超时(>5s)或报错 → 返回 HTTP 200 + 降级提示文案
423	- 使用 `try/catch` 包裹所有 AI 调用
424	- 降级不影响系统其他功能

428	## 八、安全与权限
429	
430	### 8.1 认证
431	- NextAuth.js JWT session
432	- 密码 bcrypt 加密存储
433	- 默认账号见 Mock 数据文档
434	
435	### 8.2 授权 (Middleware + Service 层双重校验)
436	- Middleware: 路由级角色校验 (拦截未匹配角色访问 /it/* /exec/*)
437	- Service: 数据级校验 (API 返回前剥离价格字段、校验跨部门操作)
438	
439	#### 8.2.1 代审批机制
440	- IT 管理员可代为审批处于 `pending_manager_1` 或 `pending_manager_2` 的工单
441	- 代审批需在 approve API 请求中传 `deputy: true`
442	- 只有 IT_ADMIN 角色可执行代审批
443	- 审计日志记录 `metadata.deputy = true, deputyOperatorId = <IT管理员ID>`
444	- 工单上 `deputyApproval = true`, `deputyApproverId` 记录代审批人
445	
446	#### 8.2.2 丢失资产自动取消关联工单
447	- 调用 `POST /api/it/assets/:id/lost` 标记丢失时，在**同一事务**内:
448	  1. 资产状态变更为 `lost`
449	  2. 查询该资产所有活跃工单(非 completed/cancelled/rejected)
450	  3. 将所有活跃工单状态变更为 `cancelled`
451	  4. 记录审计日志(资产状态变更 + 工单取消)
452	- 以上四步原子执行，任一步失败则全部回滚
453	
454	### 8.3 审计
455	- `AuditLog` 表只 INSERT，不提供 UPDATE/DELETE API
456	- 关键操作自动记录: 工单状态变更、资产状态变更、AI 调用

462	```dockerfile

464	FROM node:22-alpine
465	WORKDIR /app
466	COPY . .
467	RUN npm ci && npm run build

471	COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/

476	
477	```bash
478	# docker-entrypoint.sh — 启动即用，无需手动预处理
479	#!/bin/sh
480	set -e
481	
482	# 等待 PostgreSQL 就绪 (最多等60秒，每2秒检查一次)
483	MAX_RETRIES=30
484	echo "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
485	for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_RETRIES); do
486	  if npx prisma db execute --stdin > /dev/null 2>&1 <<< "SELECT 1"; then
487	    echo "PostgreSQL is ready."
488	    break
489	  fi
490	  if [ "$i" = "$MAX_RETRIES" ]; then
491	    echo "ERROR: PostgreSQL did not become ready within $((MAX_RETRIES * 2)) seconds."
492	    exit 1
493	  fi
494	  echo "  ...waiting ($i/$MAX_RETRIES)"
495	  sleep 2
496	done
497	
498	# 数据库迁移
499	echo "Running database migrations..."
500	npx prisma migrate deploy
501	
502	# 幂等 seed (seed脚本内使用 INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING 确保可重复执行)
503	echo "Seeding database..."
504	npx prisma db seed
505	
506	echo "Starting application..."
507	exec "$@"
508	```

591	## 十二、AC 验收标准逐条对照
592	
593	### 🔴 红线 (一票否决)
594	| 编号 | 描述 | 设计方案 | 状态 |
595	|---|---|---|---|
596	| RL-01 | 已报废资产不能被申领 | 状态机 lost/scrapped 为终止态，分配资产前校验 status=available | ✓ |
597	| RL-02 | 越权访问他人资产被拒 | Service 层按角色+部门过滤，非本部门+非本人返回404 | ✓ |
598	| RL-03 | 审计日志不可删除修改 | AuditLog 表只INSERT，API 不暴露 UPDATE/DELETE | ✓ |
599	| RL-04 | 价格信息对员工保密 | §5.2 DTO 剥离规则 + AI 对话助手遵循权限表 | ✓ |
600	
601	### 🟦 功能主流程 (FN-01~15)
602	| 编号 | 关键设计 |
603	|---|---|
604	| FN-01 完整申领 | apply API → 审批链(含跳过规则) → assign API → confirm API |
605	| FN-02 低价跳过主管 | 审批链校验: 单价<1000 → 跳过 PENDING_MANAGER_1 |
606	| FN-03 主管不能自批 | 审批校验: applicantId === approverId 且 role=MANAGER → 跳过该节点 |
607	| FN-04 归还流程 | return API → IT confirm API → 资产状态 returning→available |
608	| FN-05 报修硬件 | repair API(AI分诊) → accept API(IT接单) → complete API |
609	| FN-06 报修软件 | AI分诊 need_repair=false → 返回自助建议，不创建工单 |
610	| FN-07 维修归还 | complete API type=return_holder → repairing→in_use |
611	| FN-08 维修报废 | complete API type=scrap → repairing→scrapped |
612	| FN-09 调拨流程 | transfer API → 双主管审批 → assign API → transferring→in_use |
613	| FN-10 扫码入库 | inbound API 创建资产(in_stock) → accept API 验收(available) |
614	| FN-11 编号唯一性 | asset.id UNIQUE 约束 + inbound API 查重 |
615	| FN-12 AI推荐 | §7.1 申领推荐 Design |
616	| FN-13 AI对话查资产 | §7.3 对话助手意图处理 |
617	| FN-14 AI二次确认 | §7.3 confirmation_required 状态机 |
618	| FN-15 审批通知 | §10 通知模板 + 工单状态变更时自动触发 |
619	
620	### 🟨 状态机 (SM-01~08)
621	| 编号 | 关键设计 |
622	|---|---|
623	| SM-01 非法转移拒绝 | AssetStateMachine.allowedTransitions 白名单校验 |
624	| SM-02 维修中不可调拨 | transfer API 前置校验: status !== repairing |
625	| SM-03 丢失不可恢复 | lost 在 allowedTransitions 中无出边 |
626	| SM-04 拒收回available | cancel API 触发 assigned→available |
627	| SM-05 驳回不扣库存 | reject API 不变更资产状态/库存 |
628	| SM-06 不能重复归还 | return API 前置校验: 无活跃归还工单 |
629	| SM-07 审批顺序 | TicketWorkflow 状态机按序校验 |
630	| SM-08 已完成不可操作 | 工单 status=completed/rejected/cancelled → 拒绝所有操作 |
631	
632	### 🟪 权限 (PM-01~06)
633	- Middleware 拦截 /it/* (仅 IT_ADMIN) 和 /exec/* (仅 EXECUTIVE)
634	- Service 层审批校验: 主管仅本部门 + 非本人
635	
636	### 🟩 AI (AI-01~10)
637	| 编号 | 关键设计 |
638	|---|---|
639	| AI-01~03 | 申领推荐 Prompt 工程 + 返回结构化 JSON |
640	| AI-04~06 | 故障分诊 Prompt + severity=high 硬规则兜底 |
641	| AI-07 | 对话助手上下文注入用户资产+保修信息 |
642	| AI-08 | 必须调用外部 LLM API，附日志可验证 |
643	| AI-09 | try/catch 降级返回 HTTP 200 + 兜底文案 |
644	| AI-10 | 推荐/对话不能绕过业务规则(已报废/价格越权) |

content.content:
1	# 初始化 Mock 数据全集 — 给研发与 QA (第一轮)
2	
3	> **说明**：这是为第一轮迭代专门准备的初始化 Mock 数据。研发应在数据库初始化或提供重置接口 `/api/test/reset` 时加载本套数据。
4	> **作用**：帮助前端选手在冷启动时立刻看到非空的 UI 界面，同时为 QA 提供执行自动化验收标准（AC）的基础基准池。
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 一、组织架构与用户基础数据
9	
10	为了满足权限校验（四种角色）及跨部门调拨审批场景，需预设以下架构：
11	
12	**部门 (Departments)**
13	- `DEPT-01`：研发部
14	- `DEPT-02`：产品部
15	- `DEPT-03`：运营部
16	
17	**用户 (Users)**
18	| 账号 (Email) | 姓名 | 部门 | 角色层级 |
19	|---|---|---|---|
20	| `employee1@company.com` | 员工甲 | 研发部 | 普通员工 |
21	| `employee2@company.com` | 员工乙 | 产品部 | 普通员工 |
22	| `manager1@company.com` | 主管甲 | 研发部 | 部门主管 |
23	| `manager2@company.com` | 主管乙 | 产品部 | 部门主管 |
24	| `admin@company.com` | 管理员 | IT部 | IT 管理员 |
25	| `exec@company.com` | 高管 | 总办 | 管理层 |
26	> *开发提示：密码可统一 Mock 为 `123456` 或跳过强制密码强度校验以方便测试。*
27	
28	---
29	
30	## 二、型号主数据 (SKU)
31	
32	| 型号 ID | 类别 | 品牌名称 | 单价 (元) | 保修期 (月) | 测试意义 |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| `MODEL-L01` | laptop | MacBook Pro M3 14寸 | 16,000 | 12 | 高价值核心资产流转测试 |
35	| `MODEL-M01` | monitor | Dell U2722D 27寸 4K | 3,200 | 36 | 常规固定资产测试 |
36	| `MODEL-P01` | peripheral | 罗技 MX Keys 无线键盘 | **699** | 24 | **<1000元，用于测试跳过主管审批直达 IT 的核心规则 (FN-02)** |
37	
38	---
39	
40	## 三、资产实例清单 (覆盖 10 大核心状态)
41	
42	这批数据精心设计了各种生命周期状态，用于测试状态机（SM）的严密性与界面的过滤功能。
43	
44	| 资产编号 (ID) | 型号关联 | 采购价 (对员工保密) | 入库相对天数 | 当前状态 | 持有人 | 测试意义 |
45	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
46	| `AST-101` | `MODEL-L01` | 16,000 | `-10天` | **in_stock** | 无 | 测试扫码入库后的待验收确认列表 |
47	| `AST-102` | `MODEL-L01` | 16,000 | `-30天` | **available** | 无 | 可用库存，用于被申领分配 |
48	| `AST-103` | `MODEL-M01` | 3,200 | `-60天` | **assigned** | employee1 | 测试员工待领用确认界面 |
49	| `AST-104` | `MODEL-L01` | 16,000 | `-100天` | **in_use** | employee2 | 员工日常持有，用于测试发起归还/报修 |
50	| `AST-105` | `MODEL-L01` | 16,000 | `-120天` | **returning** | employee1 | 测试 IT 确认接收并入库流程 |
51	| `AST-106` | `MODEL-M01` | 3,200 | `-200天` | **repairing** | employee2 | 维修中资产，验证不可调拨规则 (SM-02) |
52	| `AST-107` | `MODEL-L01` | 16,000 | `-150天` | **transferring**| manager1 | 测试跨部门主管双重审批节点 |
53	| `AST-108` | `MODEL-M01` | 3,200 | `-300天` | **scrapped** | 无 | 测试已报废资产不可重新申领红线 (RL-01) |
54	| `AST-109` | `MODEL-P01` | 699 | `-400天` | **lost** | 无 | 测试丢失资产彻底锁定 |
55	| `AST-110` | `MODEL-P01` | 699 | `-5天` | **available** | 无 | 小金额资产，用于被低价免审批工单分配 |
56	
57	---
58	
59	## 四、动态流转工单数据 (极大降低 UI 联调成本)
60	
61	为解决前端冷启动时页面一片空白（空状态）的问题，预埋以下正在流转中的业务工单。
62	
63	| 工单 ID | 类型 | 关联资产 | 申请人 | 状态 | 当前停留节点 | 测试目标 |
64	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
65	| `TKT-A01` | 申领 | (待分配) | employee1 | **pending_manager** | manager1 (主管甲) | 主管登录后，能看到待办列表中有一条来自部门员工的申领单，用于测试“通过/驳回”按钮弹窗。 |
66	| `TKT-A02` | 申领 | (待分配) | employee2 | **pending_it** | admin (管理员) | 管理员能看到待处理单，用于测试 IT “分配实体资产” (将 `AST-102` 分配给它) 的流程。 |
67	| `TKT-R01` | 报修 | `AST-106` | employee2 | **pending_it** | admin (管理员) | 用于管理员接单、并在修好后将资产转为 `in_use` 或 `scrapped`。 |
68	| `TKT-T01` | 调拨 | `AST-107` | admin | **transferring** | manager2 (接收方) | 测试跨部门调拨。由 manager1 调拨给 manager2，验证状态机逻辑。 |
69	
70	---
71	
72	## 五、消息与通知测试数据
73	
74	预设几条未读消息，以便测试顶部的“小红点”与消息列表界面。
75	
76	| 通知 ID | 接收方 | 状态 | 通知内容模版 | 关联跳转对象 |
77	|---|---|---|---|---|
78	| `MSG-001` | employee1 | 未读 | 您的申领工单已通过审批，请前往 IT 前台领取设备。 | `TKT-A02` (或历史完成的某单) |
79	| `MSG-002` | manager1 | 未读 | 您有新的待办：员工甲提交了笔记本电脑申领，请及时审批。 | `TKT-A01` |
80	| `MSG-003` | employee2 | 未读 | 【预警】您持有的 `MacBook Pro M3` (AST-104) 保修期即将于 7 天后到期。 | `AST-104` |
81	
82	---
83	
84	*— End of Document —*

1 --- 2 title: Route Handlers 3 nav_title: Route Handlers 4 description: Learn how to use Route Handlers 5 related: 6 title: API Reference 7 description: Learn more about Route Handlers 8 links: 9 - app/api-reference/file-conventions/route 10 - app/guides/backend-for-frontend 11 --- 12 13 ## Route Handlers 14 15 Route Handlers allow you to create custom request handlers for a given route using the Web [Request](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) and [Response](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Response) APIs. 16 17 24 25 > **Good to know**: Route Handlers are only available inside the `app` directory. They are the equivalent of [API Routes](/docs/pages/building-your-application/routing/api-routes) inside the `pages` directory meaning you **do not** need to use API Routes and Route Handlers together. 26 27 ### Convention 28 29 Route Handlers are defined in a [`route.js|ts` file](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route) inside the `app` directory: 30 31 ```ts filename="app/api/route.ts" switcher 32 export async function GET(request: Request) {} 33 ``` 34 35 ```js filename="app/api/route.js" switcher 36 export async function GET(request) {} 37 ``` 38 39 Route Handlers can be nested anywhere inside the `app` directory, similar to `page.js` and `layout.js`. But there **cannot** be a `route.js` file at the same route segment level as `page.js`. 40 41 ### Supported HTTP Methods 42 43 The following [HTTP methods](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Methods) are supported: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS`. If an unsupported method is called, Next.js will return a `405 Method Not Allowed` response. 44 45 ### Extended `NextRequest` and `NextResponse` APIs 46 47 In addition to supporting the native [Request](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) and [Response](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Response) APIs, Next.js extends them with [`NextRequest`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/next-request) and [`NextResponse`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/next-response) to provide convenient helpers for advanced use cases. 48 49 ### Caching 50 51 Route Handlers are not cached by default. You can, however, opt into caching for `GET` methods. Other supported HTTP methods are **not** cached. To cache a `GET` method, use a [route config option](/docs/app/guides/caching-without-cache-components#dynamic) such as `export const dynamic = 'force-static'` in your Route Handler file. 52 53 ```ts filename="app/items/route.ts" switcher 54 export const dynamic = 'force-static' 55 56 export async function GET() { 57 const res = await fetch('https://data.mongodb-api.com/...', { 58 headers: { 59 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 60 'API-Key': process.env.DATA_API_KEY, 61 }, 62 }) 63 const data = await res.json() 64 65 return Response.json({ data }) 66 } 67 ``` 68 69 ```js filename="app/items/route.js" switcher 70 export const dynamic = 'force-static' 71 72 export async function GET() { 73 const res = await fetch('https://data.mongodb-api.com/...', { 74 headers: { 75 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 76 'API-Key': process.env.DATA_API_KEY, 77 }, 78 }) 79 const data = await res.json() 80 81 return Response.json({ data }) 82 } 83 ``` 84 85 > **Good to know**: Other supported HTTP methods are **not** cached, even if they are placed alongside a `GET` method that is cached, in the same file. 86 87 #### With Cache Components 88 89 When [Cache Components](/docs/app/getting-started/caching) is enabled, `GET` Route Handlers follow the same model as normal UI routes in your application. They run at request time by default, can be prerendered when they don't access uncached or runtime data, and you can use `use cache` to include uncached data in the static response. 90 91 **Static example** - doesn't access uncached or runtime data, so it will be prerendered at build time: 92 93 ```tsx filename="app/api/project-info/route.ts" 94 export async function GET() { 95 return Response.json({ 96 projectName: 'Next.js', 97 }) 98 } 99 ``` 100 101 **Dynamic example** - accesses non-deterministic operations. During the build, prerendering stops when `Math.random()` is called, deferring to request-time rendering: 102 103 ```tsx filename="app/api/random-number/route.ts" 104 export async function GET() { 105 return Response.json({ 106 randomNumber: Math.random(), 107 }) 108 } 109 ``` 110 111 **Runtime data example** - accesses request-specific data. Prerendering terminates when runtime APIs like `headers()` are called: 112 113 ```tsx filename="app/api/user-agent/route.ts" 114 import { headers } from 'next/headers' 115 116 export async function GET() { 117 const headersList = await headers() 118 const userAgent = headersList.get('user-agent') 119 120 return Response.json({ userAgent }) 121 } 122 ``` 123 124 > **Good to know**: Prerendering stops if the `GET` handler accesses network requests, database queries, async file system operations, request object properties (like `req.url`, `request.headers`, `request.cookies`, `request.body`), runtime APIs like [`cookies()`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies), [`headers()`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/headers), [`connection()`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/connection), or non-deterministic operations. 125 126 **Cached example** - accesses uncached data (database query) but caches it with `use cache`, allowing it to be included in the prerendered response: 127 128 ```tsx filename="app/api/products/route.ts" 129 import { cacheLife } from 'next/cache' 130 131 export async function GET() { 132 const products = await getProducts() 133 return Response.json(products) 134 } 135 136 async function getProducts() { 137 'use cache' 138 cacheLife('hours') 139 140 return await db.query('SELECT * FROM products') 141 } 142 ``` 143 144 > **Good to know**: `use cache` cannot be used directly inside a Route Handler body; extract it to a helper function. Cached responses revalidate according to `cacheLife` when a new request arrives. 145 146 ### Special Route Handlers 147 148 Special Route Handlers like [`sitemap.ts`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/metadata/sitemap), [`opengraph-image.tsx`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/metadata/opengraph-image), and [`icon.tsx`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/metadata/app-icons), and other [metadata files](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/metadata) remain static by default unless they use Request-time APIs or dynamic config options. 149 150 ### Route Resolution 151 152 You can consider a `route` the lowest level routing primitive. 153 154 - They **do not** participate in layouts or client-side navigations like `page`. 155 - There **cannot** be a `route.js` file at the same route as `page.js`. 156 157 | Page | Route | Result | 158 | -------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------------- | 159 | `app/page.js` | `app/route.js` | Conflict | 160 | `app/page.js` | `app/api/route.js` | Valid | 161 | `app/[user]/page.js` | `app/api/route.js` | Valid | 162 163 Each `route.js` or `page.js` file takes over all HTTP verbs for that route. 164 165 ```ts filename="app/page.ts" switcher 166 export default function Page() { 167 return

Hello, Next.js!

168 } 169 170 // Conflict 171 // `app/route.ts` 172 export async function POST(request: Request) {} 173 ``` 174 175 ```js filename="app/page.js" switcher 176 export default function Page() { 177 return

Hello, Next.js!

178 } 179 180 // Conflict 181 // `app/route.js` 182 export async function POST(request) {} 183 ``` 184 185 Read more about how Route Handlers [complement your frontend application](/docs/app/guides/backend-for-frontend), or explore the Route Handlers [API Reference](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route). 186 187 ### Route Context Helper 188 189 In TypeScript, you can type the `context` parameter for Route Handlers with the globally available [`RouteContext`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route#route-context-helper) helper: 190 191 ```ts filename="app/users/[id]/route.ts" switcher 192 import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server' 193 194 export async function GET(_req: NextRequest, ctx: RouteContext<'/users/[id]'>) { 195 const { id } = await ctx.params 196 return Response.json({ id }) 197 } 198 ``` 199 200 > **Good to know** 201 > 202 > - Types are generated during `next dev`, `next build` or `next typegen`. 203

While you can implement a custom auth solution, for increased security and simplicity, we recommend using an authentication library. These offer built-in solutions for authentication, session management, and authorization, as well as additional features such as social logins, multi-factor authentication, and role-based access control. Create a user or check user credentials 302 303 After validating form fields, you can create a new user account or check if the user exists by calling your authentication provider's API or database. 304 305 Continuing from the previous example: 306 307 ```tsx filename="app/actions/auth.tsx" switcher 308 export async function signup(state: FormState, formData: FormData) { 309 // 1. Create user session 371 // 5. Redirect user 372 } 373 ``` 374 375 After successfully creating the user account or verifying the user credentials, you can create a session to manage the user's auth state. Depending on your session management strategy, the session can be stored in a cookie or database, or both. Continue to the [Session Management](#session-management) section to learn more. 376 377 > **Tips:** 378 > 379 > - The example above is verbose since it breaks down the authentication steps for the purpose of education. This highlights that implementing your own secure solution can quickly become complex. Consider using an [Auth Library](#auth-libraries) to simplify the process. 380 > - To improve the user experience, you may want to check for duplicate emails or usernames earlier in the registration flow. For example, as the user types in a username or the input field loses focus. This can help prevent unnecessary form submissions and provide immediate feedback to the user. You can debounce requests with libraries such as [use-debounce](https://www.npmjs.com/package/use-debounce) to manage the frequency of these checks. 381 382 383 384 385 386 Here are the steps to implement a sign-up and/or login form: 387 388 1. The user submits their credentials through a form. 389 2. The form sends a request that is handled by an API route. 390 3. Upon successful verification, the process is completed, indicating the user's successful authentication. 391 4. If verification is unsuccessful, an error message is shown. 392 393 Consider a login form where users can input their credentials: 394 395 ```tsx filename="pages/login.tsx" switcher 396 import { FormEvent } from 'react' 397 import { useRouter } from 'next/router' 398 399 export default function LoginPage() { 400 const router = useRouter() 401 402 async function handleSubmit(event: FormEvent) { 403 event.preventDefault() 404 405 const formData = new FormData(event.currentTarget) 406 const email = formData.get('email') 407 const password = formData.get('password') 408 409 const response = await fetch('/api/auth/login', { 410 method: 'POST', 411 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, 412 body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }), 413 }) 414 415 if (response.ok) { 416 router.push('/profile') 417 } else { 418 // Handle errors 419 } 420 } 421 422 return ( 423
424 425 426 427
428 ) 429 } 430 ``` 431 432 ```jsx filename="pages/login.jsx" switcher 433 import { FormEvent } from 'react' 434 import { useRouter } from 'next/router' 435 436 export default function LoginPage() { 437 const router = useRouter() 438 439 async function handleSubmit(event) { 440 event.preventDefault() 441 442 const formData = new FormData(event.currentTarget) 443 const email = formData.get('email') 444 const password = formData.get('password') 445 446 const response = await fetch('/api/auth/login', { 447 method: 'POST', 448 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, 449 body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }), 450 }) 451 452 if (response.ok) { 453 router.push('/profile') 454 } else { 455 // Handle errors 456 } 457 } 458 459 return ( 460
461 462 463 464
465 ) 466 } 467 ``` 468 469 The form above has two input fields for capturing the user's email and password. On submission, it triggers a function that sends a POST request to an API route (`/api/auth/login`). 470 471 You can then call your Authentication Provider's API in the API route to handle authentication: 472 473 ```ts filename="pages/api/auth/login.ts" switcher 474 import type { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next' 475 import { signIn } from '@/auth' 476 477 export default async function handler( 478 req: NextApiRequest, 479 res: NextApiResponse 480 ) { 481 try { 482 const { email, password } = req.body 483 await signIn('credentials', { email, password }) 484 485 res.status(200).json({ success: true }) 486 } catch (error) { 487 if (error.type === 'CredentialsSignin') { 488 res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials.' }) 489 } else { 490 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Something went wrong.' }) 491 } 492 } 493 } 494 ``` 495 496 ```js filename="pages/api/auth/login.js" switcher 497 import { signIn } from '@/auth' 498 499 export default async function handler(req, res) { 500 try { 501 const { email, password } = req.body 502 await signIn('credentials', { email, password }) 503 504 res.status(200).json({ success: true }) 505 } catch (error) { 506 if (error.type === 'CredentialsSignin') { 507 res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials.' }) 508 } else { 509 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Something went wrong.' }) 510 } 511 } 512 } 513 ``` 514 515 516 517 ## Session Management 518 519 Session management ensures that the user's authenticated state is preserved across requests. It involves creating, storing, refreshing, and deleting sessions or tokens. 520 521 There are two types of sessions: 522 523 1. [**Stateless**](#stateless-sessions): Session data (or a token) is stored in the browser's cookies. The cookie is sent with each request, allowing the session to be verified on the server. This method is simpler, but can be less secure if not implemented correctly. 524 2. [**Database**](#database-sessions): Session data is stored in a database, with the user's browser only receiving the encrypted session ID. This method is more secure, but can be complex and use more server resources. 525 526 > **Good to know:** While you can use either method, or both, we recommend using a session management library such as [iron-session](https://github.com/vvo/iron-session) or [Jose](https://github.com/panva/jose). 527 528 ### Stateless Sessions 529 530 531 532 To create and manage stateless sessions, there are a few steps you need to follow: 533 534 1. Generate a secret key, which will be used to sign your session, and store it as an [environment variable](/docs/app/guides/environment-variables). 535 2. Write logic to encrypt/decrypt session data using a session management library. 536 3. Manage cookies using the Next.js [`cookies`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies) API. 537 538 In addition to the above, consider adding functionality to [update (or refresh)](#updating-or-refreshing-sessions) the session when the user returns to the application, and [delete](#deleting-the-session) the session when the user logs out. 539 540 > **Good to know:** Check if your [auth library](#auth-libraries) includes session management. 541 542 #### 1. Generating a secret key 543 544 There are a few ways you can generate secret key to sign your session. For example, you may choose to use the `openssl` command in your terminal: 545 546 ```bash filename="terminal" 547 openssl rand -base64 32 548 ``` 549 550 This command generates a 32-character random string that you can use as your secret key and store in your [environment variables file](/docs/app/guides/environment-variables): 551 552 ```bash filename=".env" 553 SESSION_SECRET=your_secret_key 554 ``` 555 556 You can then reference this key in your session management logic: 557 558 ```js filename="app/lib/session.js" 559 const secretKey = process.env.SESSION_SECRET 560 ``` 561 562 #### 2. Encrypting and decrypting sessions 563 564 Next, you can use your preferred [session management library](#session-management-libraries) to encrypt and decrypt sessions. Continuing from the previous example, we'll use [Jose](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jose) (compatible with the [Edge Runtime](/docs/app/api-reference/edge)) and React's [`server-only`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/server-only) package to ensure that your session management logic is only executed on the server. 565 566 ```tsx filename="app/lib/session.ts" switcher 567 import 'server-only' 568 import { SignJWT, jwtVerify } from 'jose' 569 import { SessionPayload } from '@/app/lib/definitions' 570 571 const secretKey = process.env.SESSION_SECRET 572 const encodedKey = new TextEncoder().encode(secretKey) 573 574 export async function encrypt(payload: SessionPayload) { 575 return new SignJWT(payload) 576 .setProtectedHeader({ alg: 'HS256' }) 577 .setIssuedAt() 578 .setExpirationTime('7d') 579 .sign(encodedKey) 580 } 581 582 export async function decrypt(session: string | undefined = '') { 583 try { 584 const { payload } = await jwtVerify(session, encodedKey, { 585 algorithms: ['HS256'], 586 }) 587 return payload 588 } catch (error) { 589 console.log('Failed to verify session') 590 } 591 } 592 ``` 593 594 ```jsx filename="app/lib/session.js" switcher 595 import 'server-only' 596 import { SignJWT, jwtVerify } from 'jose' 597 598 const secretKey = process.env.SESSION_SECRET 599 const encodedKey = new TextEncoder().encode(secretKey) 600 601 export async function encrypt(payload) { 602 return new SignJWT(payload) 603 .setProtectedHeader({ alg: 'HS256' }) 604 .setIssuedAt() 605 .setExpirationTime('7d') 606 .sign(encodedKey) 607 } 608 609 export async function decrypt(session) { 610 try { 611 const { payload } = await jwtVerify(session, encodedKey, { 612 algorithms: ['HS256'], 613 }) 614 return payload 615 } catch (error) { 616 console.log('Failed to verify session') 617 } 618 } 619 ``` 620 621 > **Tips**: 622 > 623 > - The payload should contain the **minimum**, unique user data that'll be used in subsequent requests, such as the user's ID, role, etc. It should not contain personally identifiable information like phone number, email address, credit card information, etc, or sensitive data like passwords. 624 625 #### 3. Setting cookies (recommended options) 626 627 To store the session in a cookie, use the Next.js [`cookies`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies) API. The cookie should be set on the server, and include the recommended options: 628 629 - **HttpOnly**: Prevents client-side JavaScript from accessing the cookie. 630 - **Secure**: Use https to send the cookie. 631 - **SameSite**: Specify whether the cookie can be sent with cross-site requests. 632 - **Max-Age or Expires**: Delete the cookie after a certain period. 633 - **Path**: Define the URL path for the cookie. 634 635 Please refer to [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Cookies) for more information on each of these options. 636 637 ```ts filename="app/lib/session.ts" switcher 638 import 'server-only' 639 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 640 641 export async function createSession(userId: string) { 642 const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) 643 const session = await encrypt({ userId, expiresAt }) 644 const cookieStore = await cookies() 645 646 cookieStore.set('session', session, { 647 httpOnly: true, 648 secure: true, 649 expires: expiresAt, 650 sameSite: 'lax', 651 path: '/', 652 }) 653 } 654 ``` 655 656 ```js filename="app/lib/session.js" switcher 657 import 'server-only' 658 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 659 660 export async function createSession(userId) { 661 const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) 662 const session = await encrypt({ userId, expiresAt }) 663 const cookieStore = await cookies() 664 665 cookieStore.set('session', session, { 666 httpOnly: true, 667 secure: true, 668 expires: expiresAt, 669 sameSite: 'lax', 670 path: '/', 671 }) 672 } 673 ``` 674 675 Back in your Server Action, you can invoke the `createSession()` function, and use the [`redirect()`](/docs/app/guides/redirecting) API to redirect the user to the appropriate page: 676 677 ```ts filename="app/actions/auth.ts" switcher 678 import { createSession } from '@/app/lib/session' 679 680 export async function signup(state: FormState, formData: FormData) { 681 // Previous steps: 682 // 1. Validate form fields 683 // 2. Prepare data for insertion into database 684 // 3. Insert the user into the database or call an Library API 685 686 // Current steps: 687 // 4. Create user session 688 await createSession(user.id) 689 // 5. Redirect user 690 redirect('/profile') 691 } 692 ``` 693 694 ```js filename="app/actions/auth.js" switcher 695 import { createSession } from '@/app/lib/session' 696 697 export async function signup(state, formData) { 698 // Previous steps: 699 // 1. Validate form fields 700 // 2. Prepare data for insertion into database 701 // 3. Insert the user into the database or call an Library API 702 703 // Current steps: 704 // 4. Create user session 705 await createSession(user.id) 706 // 5. Redirect user 707 redirect('/profile') 708 } 709 ``` 710 711 > **Tips**: 712 > 713 > - **Cookies should be set on the server** to prevent client-side tampering. 714 > - 🎥 Watch: Learn more about stateless sessions and authentication with Next.js → [YouTube (11 minutes)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DJvM2lSPn6w). 715 716 #### Updating (or refreshing) sessions 717 718 You can also extend the session's expiration time. This is useful for keeping the user logged in after they access the application again. Create a table in your database to store session and data (or check if your Auth Library handles this). 873 2. Implement functionality to insert, update, and delete sessions 874 3. Encrypt the session ID before storing it in the user's browser, and ensure the database and cookie stay in sync (this is optional, but recommended for optimistic auth checks in [Proxy](#optimistic-checks-with-proxy-optional)). 875 876 877 878 For example: 879 880 ```ts filename="app/lib/session.ts" switcher 881 import cookies from 'next/headers' 882 import { db } from '@/app/lib/db' 883 import { encrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 884 885 export async function createSession(id: number) { 886 const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) 887 888 // 1. Create a session in the database 889 const data = await db 890 .insert(sessions) 891 .values({ 892 userId: id, 893 expiresAt, 894 }) 895 // Return the session ID 896 .returning({ id: sessions.id }) 897 898 const sessionId = data[0].id 899 900 // 2. Encrypt the session ID 901 const session = await encrypt({ sessionId, expiresAt }) 902 903 // 3. Store the session in cookies for optimistic auth checks 904 const cookieStore = await cookies() 905 cookieStore.set('session', session, { 906 httpOnly: true, 907 secure: true, 908 expires: expiresAt, 909 sameSite: 'lax', 910 path: '/', 911 }) 912 } 913 ``` 914 915 ```js filename="app/lib/session.js" switcher 916 import cookies from 'next/headers' 917 import { db } from '@/app/lib/db' 918 import { encrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 919 920 export async function createSession(id) { 921 const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) 922 923 // 1. Create a session in the database 924 const data = await db 925 .insert(sessions) 926 .values({ 927 userId: id, 928 expiresAt, 929 }) 930 // Return the session ID 931 .returning({ id: sessions.id }) 932 933 const sessionId = data[0].id 934 935 // 2. Encrypt the session ID 936 const session = await encrypt({ sessionId, expiresAt }) 937 938 // 3. Store the session in cookies for optimistic auth checks 939 const cookieStore = await cookies() 940 cookieStore.set('session', session, { 941 httpOnly: true, 942 secure: true, 943 expires: expiresAt, 944 sameSite: 'lax', 945 path: '/', 946 }) 947 } 948 ``` 949 950 > **Tips**: 951 > 952 > - For faster access, you may consider adding server caching for the lifetime of the session. You can also keep the session data in your primary database, and combine data requests to reduce the number of queries. 953 > - You may opt to use database sessions for more advanced use cases, such as keeping track of the last time a user logged in, or number of active devices, or give users the ability to log out of all devices. 954 955 After implementing session management, you'll need to add authorization logic to control what users can access and do within your application. Continue to the [Authorization](#authorization) section to learn more. 956 957 958 959 960 961 **Creating a Session on the Server**: 962 963 ```ts filename="pages/api/create-session.ts" switcher 964 import db from '../../lib/db' 965 import type { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next' 966 967 export default async function handler( 968 req: NextApiRequest, 969 res: NextApiResponse 970 ) { 971 try { 972 const user = req.body 973 const sessionId = generateSessionId() 974 await db.insertSession({ 975 sessionId, 976 userId: user.id, 977 createdAt: new Date(), 978 }) 979 980 res.status(200).json({ sessionId }) 981 } catch (error) { 982 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal Server Error' }) 983 } 984 } 985 ``` 986 987 ```js filename="pages/api/create-session.js" switcher 988 import db from '../../lib/db' 989 990 export default async function handler(req, res) { 991 try { 992 const user = req.body 993 const sessionId = generateSessionId() 994 await db.insertSession({ 995 sessionId, 996 userId: user.id, 997 createdAt: new Date(), 998 }) 999 1000 res.status(200).json({ sessionId }) 1001 } catch (error) { 1002 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal Server Error' }) 1003 } 1004 } 1005 ``` 1006 1007 1008 1009 ## Authorization 1010 1011 Once a user is authenticated and a session is created, you can implement authorization to control what the user can access and do within your application. 1012 1013 There are two main types of authorization checks: 1014 1015 1. **Optimistic**: Checks if the user is authorized to access a route or perform an action using the session data stored in the cookie. These checks are useful for quick operations, such as showing/hiding UI elements or redirecting users based on permissions or roles. 1016 2. **Secure**: Checks if the user is authorized to access a route or perform an action using the session data stored in the database. These checks are more secure and are used for operations that require access to sensitive data or actions. 1017 1018 For both cases, we recommend: 1019 1020 - Creating a [Data Access Layer](#creating-a-data-access-layer-dal) to centralize your authorization logic 1021 - Using [Data Transfer Objects (DTO)](#using-data-transfer-objects-dto) to only return the necessary data 1022 - Optionally use [Proxy](#optimistic-checks-with-proxy-optional) to perform optimistic checks. 1023 1024 ### Optimistic checks with Proxy (Optional) 1025 1026 There are some cases where you may want to use [Proxy](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy) and redirect users based on permissions: 1027 1028 - To perform optimistic checks. Since Proxy runs on every route, it's a good way to centralize redirect logic and pre-filter unauthorized users. 1029 - To protect static routes that share data between users (e.g. content behind a paywall). 1030 1031 However, since Proxy runs on every route, including [prefetched](/docs/app/getting-started/linking-and-navigating#prefetching) routes, it's important to only read the session from the cookie (optimistic checks), and avoid database checks to prevent performance issues. 1032 1033 For example: 1034 1035 ```tsx filename="proxy.ts" switcher 1036 import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server' 1037 import { decrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 1038 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 1039 1040 // 1. Specify protected and public routes 1041 const protectedRoutes = ['/dashboard'] 1042 const publicRoutes = ['/login', '/signup', '/'] 1043 1044 export default async function proxy(req: NextRequest) { 1045 // 2. Check if the current route is protected or public 1046 const path = req.nextUrl.pathname 1047 const isProtectedRoute = protectedRoutes.includes(path) 1048 const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.includes(path) 1049 1050 // 3. Decrypt the session from the cookie 1051 const cookie = (await cookies()).get('session')?.value 1052 const session = await decrypt(cookie) 1053 1054 // 4. Redirect to /login if the user is not authenticated 1055 if (isProtectedRoute && !session?.userId) { 1056 return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.nextUrl)) 1057 } 1058 1059 // 5. Redirect to /dashboard if the user is authenticated 1060 if ( 1061 isPublicRoute && 1062 session?.userId && 1063 !req.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/dashboard') 1064 ) { 1065 return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/dashboard', req.nextUrl)) 1066 } 1067 1068 return NextResponse.next() 1069 } 1070 1071 // Routes Proxy should not run on 1072 export const config = { 1073 matcher: ['/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|.*\\.png$).*)'], 1074 } 1075 ``` 1076 1077 ```js filename="proxy.js" switcher 1078 import { NextResponse } from 'next/server' 1079 import { decrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 1080 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 1081 1082 // 1. Specify protected and public routes 1083 const protectedRoutes = ['/dashboard'] 1084 const publicRoutes = ['/login', '/signup', '/'] 1085 1086 export default async function proxy(req) { 1087 // 2. Check if the current route is protected or public 1088 const path = req.nextUrl.pathname 1089 const isProtectedRoute = protectedRoutes.includes(path) 1090 const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.includes(path) 1091 1092 // 3. Decrypt the session from the cookie 1093 const cookie = (await cookies()).get('session')?.value 1094 const session = await decrypt(cookie) 1095 1096 // 5. Redirect to /login if the user is not authenticated 1097 if (isProtectedRoute && !session?.userId) { 1098 return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.nextUrl)) 1099 } 1100 1101 // 6. Redirect to /dashboard if the user is authenticated 1102 if ( 1103 isPublicRoute && 1104 session?.userId && 1105 !req.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/dashboard') 1106 ) { 1107 return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/dashboard', req.nextUrl)) 1108 } 1109 1110 return NextResponse.next() 1111 } 1112 1113 // Routes Proxy should not run on 1114 export const config = { 1115 matcher: ['/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|.*\\.png$).*)'], 1116 } 1117 ``` 1118 1119 While Proxy can be useful for initial checks, it should not be your only line of defense in protecting your data. The majority of security checks should be performed as close as possible to your data source, see [Data Access Layer](#creating-a-data-access-layer-dal) for more information. 1120 1121 > **Tips**: 1122 > 1123 > - In Proxy, you can also read cookies using `req.cookies.get('session').value`. 1124 > - Proxy uses the Node.js runtime, check if your Auth library and session management library are compatible. You may need to use [Middleware](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/v15.5.6/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/middleware.mdx) if your Auth library only supports [Edge Runtime](/docs/app/api-reference/edge) 1125 > - You can use the `matcher` property in the Proxy to specify which routes Proxy should run on. Although, for auth, it's recommended Proxy runs on all routes. 1126 1127 1128 1129 ### Creating a Data Access Layer (DAL) 1130 1131 We recommend creating a DAL to centralize your data requests and authorization logic. 1132 1133 The DAL should include a function that verifies the user's session as they interact with your application. At the very least, the function should check if the session is valid, then redirect or return the user information needed to make further requests. 1134 1135 For example, create a separate file for your DAL that includes a `verifySession()` function. Then use React's [cache](https://react.dev/reference/react/cache) API to memoize the return value of the function during a React render pass: 1136 1137 ```tsx filename="app/lib/dal.ts" switcher 1138 import 'server-only' 1139 1140 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 1141 import { decrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 1142 1143 export const verifySession = cache(async () => { 1144 const cookie = (await cookies()).get('session')?.value 1145 const session = await decrypt(cookie) 1146 1147 if (!session?.userId) { 1148 redirect('/login') 1149 } 1150 1151 return { isAuth: true, userId: session.userId } 1152 }) 1153 ``` 1154 1155 ```js filename="app/lib/dal.js" switcher 1156 import 'server-only' 1157 1158 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 1159 import { decrypt } from '@/app/lib/session' 1160 1161 export const verifySession = cache(async () => { 1162 const cookie = (await cookies()).get('session')?.value 1163 const session = await decrypt(cookie) 1164 1165 if (!session.userId) { 1166 redirect('/login') 1167 } 1168 1169 return { isAuth: true, userId: session.userId } 1170 }) 1171 ``` 1172 1173 You can then invoke the `verifySession()` function in your data requests, Server Actions, Route Handlers: 1174 1175 ```tsx filename="app/lib/dal.ts" switcher 1176 export const getUser = cache(async () => { 1177 const session = await verifySession() 1178 if (!session) return null 1179 1180 try { 1181 const data = await db.query.users.findMany({ 1182 where: eq(users.id, session.userId), 1183 // Explicitly return the columns you need rather than the whole user object 1184 columns: { 1185 id: true, 1186 name: true, 1187 email: true, 1188 }, 1189 }) 1190 1191 const user = data[0] 1192 1193 return user 1194 } catch (error) { 1195 console.log('Failed to fetch user') 1196 return null 1197 } 1198 }) 1199 ``` 1200 1201 ```jsx filename="app/lib/dal.js" switcher 1202 export const getUser = cache(async () => { 1203 const session = await verifySession() 1204 if (!session) return null 1205 1206 try { 1207 const data = await db.query.users.findMany({ 1208 where: eq(users.id, session.userId), 1209 // Explicitly return the columns you need rather than the whole user object 1210 columns: { 1211 id: true, 1212 name: true, 1213 email: true, 1214 }, 1215 }) 1216 1217 const user = data[0] 1218 1219 return user 1220 } catch (error) { 1221 console.log('Failed to fetch user') 1222 return null 1223 } 1224 }) 1225 ``` 1226 1227 > **Tip**: 1228 > 1229 > - A DAL can be used to protect data fetched at request time. However, for static routes that share data between users, data will be fetched at build time and not at request time. Use [Proxy](#optimistic-checks-with-proxy-optional) to protect static routes. 1230 > - For secure checks, you can check if the session is valid by comparing the session ID with your database. Use React's [cache](https://react.dev/reference/react/cache) function to avoid unnecessary duplicate requests to the database during a render pass. 1231 > - You may wish to consolidate related data requests in a JavaScript class that runs `verifySession()` before any methods. 1232 1233 ### Using Data Transfer Objects (DTO) 1234 1235 When retrieving data, it's recommended you return only the necessary data that will be used in your application, and not entire objects. For example, if you're fetching user data, you might only return the user's ID and name, rather than the entire user object which could contain passwords, phone numbers, etc. 1236 1237 However, if you have no control over the returned data structure, or are working in a team where you want to avoid whole objects being passed to the client, you can use strategies such as specifying what fields are safe to be exposed to the client. 1238 1239 ```tsx filename="app/lib/dto.ts" switcher 1240 import 'server-only' 1241 import { getUser } from '@/app/lib/dal' 1242 1243 function canSeeUsername(viewer: User) { 1244 return true 1245 } 1246 1247 function canSeePhoneNumber(viewer: User, team: string) { 1248 return viewer.isAdmin || team === viewer.team 1249 } 1250 1251 export async function getProfileDTO(slug: string) { 1252 const data = await db.query.users.findMany({ 1253 where: eq(users.slug, slug), 1254 // Return specific columns here 1255 }) 1256 const user = data[0] 1257 1258 const currentUser = await getUser(user.id) 1259 1260 // Or return only what's specific to the query here 1261 return { 1262 username: canSeeUsername(currentUser) ? user.username : null, 1263 phonenumber: canSeePhoneNumber(currentUser, user.team) 1264 ? user.phonenumber 1265 : null, 1266 } 1267 } 1268 ``` 1269 1270 ```js filename="app/lib/dto.js" switcher 1271 import 'server-only' 1272 import { getUser } from '@/app/lib/dal' 1273 1274 function canSeeUsername(viewer) { 1275 return true 1276 } 1277 1278 function canSeePhoneNumber(viewer, team) { 1279 return viewer.isAdmin || team === viewer.team 1280 } 1281 1282 export async function getProfileDTO(slug) { 1283 const data = await db.query.users.findMany({ 1284 where: eq(users.slug, slug), 1285 // Return specific columns here 1286 }) 1287 const user = data[0] 1288 1289 const currentUser = await getUser(user.id) 1290 1291 // Or return only what's specific to the query here 1292 return { 1293 username: canSeeUsername(currentUser) ? user.username : null, 1294 phonenumber: canSeePhoneNumber(currentUser, user.team) 1295 ? user.phonenumber 1296 : null, 1297 } 1298 } 1299 ``` 1300 1301 By centralizing your data requests and authorization logic in a DAL and using DTOs, you can ensure that all data requests are secure and consistent, making it easier to maintain, audit, and debug as your application scales. 1302 1303 > **Good to know**: 1304 > 1305 > - There are a couple of different ways you can define a DTO, from using `toJSON()`, to individual functions like the example above, or JS classes. Since these are JavaScript patterns and not a React or Next.js feature, we recommend doing some research to find the best pattern for your application. 1306 > - Learn more about security best practices in our [Security in Next.js article](/blog/security-nextjs-server-components-actions). 1307 1308 ### Server Components 1309 1310 Auth check in [Server Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components) are useful for role-based access. enum NotificationType {
```prisma
  APPROVAL_REQUEST
  APPROVAL_RESULT
  PICKUP_READY
  REPAIR_UPDATE
  WARRANTY_EXPIRY
  TRANSFER_REQUEST
  RETURN_SUBMITTED
}

enum InventorySessionStatus {
  IN_PROGRESS
  COMPLETED

// Models
model Department {
  id        String  @id
  name      String
  managerId String? @map("manager_id")

  manager  User?          @relation("DepartmentManager", fields: [managerId], references: [id])
  users    User[]         @relation("UserDepartment")
  assets   AssetInstance[] @relation("AssetDepartment")
  tickets  Ticket[]       @relation("TargetDepartment")

  @@map("departments")
}

model User {
  id           String     @id @default(cuid())
  email        String     @unique
  passwordHash String     @map("password_hash")
  name         String
  role         UserRole
  deptId       String     @map("dept_id")
  status       UserStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  createdAt    DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  department         Department          @relation("UserDepartment", fields: [deptId], references: [id])
  managedDepartment  Department?         @relation("DepartmentManager")
  heldAssets         AssetInstance[]     @relation("AssetHolder")
  appliedTickets     Ticket[]            @relation("Applicant")
  assignedTickets    Ticket[]            @relation("AssignedBy")
  processedTickets   Ticket[]            @relation("Processor")
  fromTransferTickets Ticket[]           @relation("FromHolder")
  toTransferTickets   Ticket[]           @relation("ToHolder")
  deputyApprovedTickets Ticket[]         @relation("DeputyApprover")
  auditLogs          AuditLog[]          @relation("Operator")
  notifications      Notification[]
  startedSessions    InventorySession[]  @relation("SessionStarter")

  @@map("users")
}

model AssetModel {
  id             String        @id
  category       AssetCategory
  brandName      String        @map("brand_name")
  modelName      String        @map("model_name")
  specs          Json
  purchasePrice  Decimal       @map("purchase_price")
  supplier       String
  warrantyMonths Int           @map("warranty_months")
  status         AssetModelStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  createdAt      DateTime      @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime      @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  instances      AssetInstance[]
  tickets        Ticket[]       @relation("TargetModel")

  @@map("asset_models")
}

model AssetInstance {
  id             String      @id
  sn             String      @unique
  modelId        String      @map("model_id")
  status         AssetStatus
  holderId       String?     @map("holder_id")
  deptId         String?     @map("dept_id")
  purchaseDate   DateTime    @map("purchase_date")
  warrantyExpiry DateTime    @map("warranty_expiry")
  scrapReason    String?     @map("scrap_reason")
  lostReason     String?     @map("lost_reason")
  createdAt      DateTime    @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime    @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  model          AssetModel     @relation(fields: [modelId], references: [id])
  holder         User?          @relation("AssetHolder", fields: [holderId], references: [id])
  department     Department?    @relation("AssetDepartment", fields: [deptId], references: [id])
  tickets        Ticket[]       @relation("TargetAsset")
  notifications  Notification[] @relation("RelatedAsset")
  inventoryScans InventoryScan[]

  @@map("asset_instances")
}

model Ticket {
  id               String       @id @default(cuid())
  type             TicketType
  status           TicketStatus
  applicantId      String       @map("applicant_id")
  targetAssetId    String?      @map("target_asset_id")
  targetModelId    String?      @map("target_model_id")
  reason           String       @db.Text
  rejectReason     String?      @map("reject_reason")
  aiTriageResult   Json?        @map("ai_triage_result")
  aiRecommendation Json?        @map("ai_recommendation")
  forceRepair      Boolean      @default(false) @map("force_repair")
  assignedById     String?      @map("assigned_by_id")
  processorId      String?      @map("processor_id")
  fromHolderId     String?      @map("from_holder_id")
  toHolderId       String?      @map("to_holder_id")
  targetDeptId     String?      @map("target_dept_id")
  deputyApproval   Boolean      @default(false) @map("deputy_approval")
  deputyApproverId String?      @map("deputy_approver_id")
  completedAt      DateTime?    @map("completed_at")
  createdAt        DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt        DateTime     @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  applicant         User         @relation("Applicant", fields: [applicantId], references: [id])
  targetAsset       AssetInstance? @relation("TargetAsset", fields: [targetAssetId], references: [id])
  targetModel       AssetModel?   @relation("TargetModel", fields: [targetModelId], references: [id])
  assignedBy        User?         @relation("AssignedBy", fields: [assignedById], references: [id])
  processor         User?         @relation("Processor", fields: [processorId], references: [id])
  fromHolder        User?         @relation("FromHolder", fields: [fromHolderId], references: [id])
  toHolder          User?         @relation("ToHolder", fields: [toHolderId], references: [id])
  targetDepartment  Department?   @relation("TargetDepartment", fields: [targetDeptId], references: [id])
  deputyApprover    User?         @relation("DeputyApprover", fields: [deputyApproverId], references: [id])

  notifications Notification[]

  @@map("tickets")
}

model AuditLog {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  action        String
  operatorId    String   @map("operator_id")
  targetType    String   @map("target_type")
  targetId      String   @map("target_id")
  beforeSnapshot Json?   @map("before_snapshot")
  afterSnapshot  Json?   @map("after_snapshot")
  metadata      Json?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  operator User @relation("Operator", fields: [operatorId], references: [id])

  @@map("audit_logs")
}

model Notification {
  id              String           @id @default(cuid())
  userId          String           @map("user_id")
  type            NotificationType
  title           String
  body            String           @db.Text
  read            Boolean          @default(false)
  relatedTicketId String?          @map("related_ticket_id")
  relatedAssetId  String?          @map("related_asset_id")
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user         User            @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  relatedTicket Ticket?        @relation(fields: [relatedTicketId], references: [id])
  relatedAsset AssetInstance?  @relation("RelatedAsset", fields: [relatedAssetId], references: [id])

  @@map("notifications")
}

model InventorySession {
  id          String                @id @default(cuid())
  startedById String                @map("started_by_id")
  status      InventorySessionStatus
  startedAt   DateTime              @default(now()) @map("started_at")
  completedAt DateTime?             @map("completed_at")

  startedBy User              @relation("SessionStarter", fields: [startedById], references: [id])
  scans     InventoryScan[]

  @@map("inventory_sessions")
}

model InventoryScan {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  sessionId String   @map("session_id")
  assetId   String   @map("asset_id")
  scannedAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("scanned_at")

  session InventorySession @relation(fields: [sessionId], references: [id])
  asset   AssetInstance    @relation(fields: [assetId], references: [id])

  @@map("inventory_scans")
}
```
